mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side constraints, leading to negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are not significantly negative when industrial production contracts for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >50% of high-inflation months over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive quarterly returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between contracting real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal does not predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surging above its long-term trend indicates robust manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term market momentum.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average by 1.5%
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in >55% of instances when the industrial production threshold is crossed.
